name: Setting Design Philosophy
consequences: Prioritizing hyper-localized cultural authenticity by embedding real-world community advisors into the design process strengthens narrative credibility but risks over-fragmenting development resources across multiple distinct urban archetypes. This could delay procedural generation tooling if not abstracted early.
options: Prioritize hyper-localized cultural authenticity by embedding real-world community advisors into the design process
Create a stylized composite city by blending architectural motifs from target locations into a unified aesthetic framework
Use procedural generation to dynamically mix urban elements from source cities during runtime based on player actions
review: Hyper-localization creates resource fragmentation risks, leaving procedural consistency gaps between curated zones and algorithmically generated areas.

name: Funding Structure Composition
consequences: Securing a single major industry partner for exclusive branding integration stabilizes short-term cash flow but creates long-term dependency risks if the partner's strategic priorities shift mid-development.
options: Secure a single major industry partner for exclusive branding integration
Diversify funding through multiple mid-tier sponsors with non-exclusive partnership terms
Allocate 40% of development budget to government innovation grants requiring public engagement components
review: Single-partner reliance creates dependency risks, leaving innovation flexibility gaps when partner priorities conflict with creative vision.

name: Feature Scope Management
consequences: Implementing a modular development pipeline that prioritizes core heist mechanics first accelerates initial playtesting but may lead to narrative cohesion issues if peripheral systems are rushed later.
options: Implement a modular development pipeline that prioritizes core heist mechanics first
Maintain parallel development of all systems through cross-functional team rotations
Use player feedback loops to dynamically adjust feature scope based on early playtest data
review: Modular prioritization creates integration risks, leaving narrative coherence gaps between independently developed systems.

name: Procedural Generation Balance
consequences: Deploying AI-driven dynamic event generation increases replayability but requires significant computational resources that could delay graphical fidelity milestones.
options: Deploy AI-driven dynamic event generation for emergent storytelling
Pre-author 70% of narrative events with 30% procedural variation for consistency
Use player behavior analytics to seed procedural systems with culturally relevant scenarios
review: AI complexity creates technical debt risks, leaving graphical polish gaps when resources shift to algorithm development.

name: Multiplayer Integration Strategy
consequences: Designing shared open-world spaces with persistent player impact alters single-player narrative structure but increases server infrastructure costs.
options: Design shared open-world spaces with persistent player impact on environment and economy
Create separate multiplayer and single-player worlds with divergent progression systems
Implement event-based multiplayer interactions that reset periodically to maintain narrative control
review: Persistent impact creates technical scalability risks, leaving narrative control gaps when player actions exceed design parameters.

name: Urban Ecosystem Integration
consequences: Prioritizing architectural fidelity to LA/Detroit/Miami reduces world coherence but increases asset development workload. A unified stylized aesthetic risks alienating players seeking authentic urban experiences, while a fragmented approach may dilute the metropolis's narrative cohesion.
options: Merge distinct cityscapes through thematic zoning with transitional buffer areas
Adopt a hyper-simplified architectural language to unify disparate urban elements
Leverage procedural generation to dynamically blend city features based on player progression
review: Enhanced authenticity demands disproportionate resource allocation, leaving procedural efficiency gaps in uncharted zones

name: Criminal Economy Dynamics
consequences: Implementing real-time market fluctuations creates emergent gameplay but requires complex simulation systems. Simplified economic models improve performance but reduce player agency in shaping the underworld's financial landscape.
options: Design interdependent crime syndicate economies with player-driven price manipulation
Establish fixed black-market exchange rates to simplify financial interactions
Use NPC behavior patterns to generate organic economic activity without simulation systems
review: Real-time economics creates maintenance burdens, leaving scripted events as the only viable alternative for low-resource areas

name: NPC Behavioral Complexity
consequences: High-fidelity AI improves immersion but increases debugging requirements. Simplified decision trees allow smoother performance but result in repetitive NPC interactions that undermine the game's lived-in atmosphere.
options: Implement context-aware dialogue systems that adapt to player actions and environment
Create layered NPC routines with emergent social networks
Use procedural storytelling to generate unique NPC backstories and motivations
review: Advanced AI creates debugging bottlenecks, leaving procedural methods as the only scalable solution for non-essential characters

name: Heist Scenario Scalability
consequences: Dynamic heist generation enables endless replayability but requires robust content delivery systems. Pre-scripted heists ensure polish but limit player creativity in approaching objectives.
options: Design modular heist frameworks that combine randomized elements with fixed core mechanics
Implement player-created heist templates shared through local multiplayer
Use AI to generate narrative-driven heist scenarios based on player reputation and resources
review: Modular systems create integration challenges, leaving pre-scripted content as the only reliable option for high-profile missions

name: Vehicle Customization Depth
consequences: Extensive modification options enhance player expression but complicate balance testing. Limited customization preserves stability but reduces long-term engagement with vehicle systems.
options: Create tiered customization systems with progressively complex modification paths
Implement player-driven vehicle design workshops with real-time physics feedback
Use procedural generation to create unique vehicle variants based on player behavior
review: Tiered systems create accessibility barriers, leaving procedural methods as the only viable solution for casual players

name: Multiplayer Social Mechanics
consequences: Persistent online interactions foster community but require extensive server infrastructure. Isolated multiplayer modes maintain technical control but limit shared world-building opportunities.
options: Design hybrid multiplayer systems with shared progression across local and online sessions
Implement player-driven social contracts that shape multiplayer interactions
Create ephemeral multiplayer events that reset periodically to maintain novelty
review: Hybrid systems create technical complexity, leaving ephemeral events as the only manageable solution for low-bandwidth regions

name: Procedural Content Density
consequences: High-density procedural generation creates vast worlds but risks player disorientation. Low-density systems ensure navigability but result in repetitive environments.
options: Implement location-based content scaling that adjusts detail levels based on player proximity
Design procedural systems with built-in narrative anchors to guide exploration
Use player behavior data to dynamically adjust content distribution patterns
review: Content scaling creates implementation challenges, leaving narrative anchors as the only reliable method for high-traffic areas

name: Urban Infrastructure Simulation
consequences: Modeling city traffic patterns to influence mission availability enhances realism but increases computational load, requiring trade-offs in AI complexity. This could limit the number of concurrent NPCs or reduce dynamic event frequency during peak simulation hours.
options: Model city traffic patterns to influence mission availability
Integrate real-time weather effects on urban navigation
Design modular district layouts for scalable expansion
review: Prioritizing traffic simulation risks overloading computational resources, leaving scalability of district layouts unaddressed during high-population scenarios.

name: Narrative Branching Frequency
consequences: Implementing choice-driven story arcs with 3+ outcomes per decision deepens player immersion but demands extensive scriptwriting and testing. This could delay release timelines if branching logic conflicts with core mission structures.
options: Implement choice-driven story arcs with 3+ outcomes per decision
Limit branching to 2 major paths per storyline
Use procedural dialogue trees to generate infinite narrative variations
review: High branching complexity introduces scheduling delays, leaving procedural dialogue consistency untested across all player choices.

name: Resource Allocation Optimization
consequences: Prioritizing graphics rendering over physics simulation elevates visual fidelity but may cause unrealistic environmental interactions. This could reduce player trust in the game's simulated world mechanics.
options: Prioritize graphics rendering over physics simulation
Distribute budget evenly across all technical systems
Concentrate resources on AI behavior over visual fidelity
review: Visual priority risks mechanical inconsistency, leaving AI behavior underdeveloped in high-traffic urban zones.

name: Player Agency Threshold
consequences: Allowing unrestricted player decisions with full consequence tracking creates a dynamic world but complicates mission design. This could lead to unintended game-breaking scenarios requiring frequent patches.
options: Allow unrestricted player decisions with full consequence tracking
Set predefined moral choice boundaries with limited outcomes
Use dynamic difficulty scaling to influence player options
review: Unrestricted agency increases bug frequency, leaving difficulty scaling insufficient for extreme player behavior.

name: Technical Performance Trade-offs
consequences: Optimizing for high-end hardware with reduced cross-platform support expands graphical capabilities but limits market reach. This could alienate players on lower-spec devices seeking accessibility.
options: Optimize for high-end hardware with reduced cross-platform support
Prioritize cross-platform consistency over graphical enhancements
Implement adaptive resolution scaling based on device capabilities
review: Hardware optimization narrows audience access, leaving cross-platform play features underdeveloped for mid-tier devices.

name: Community Content Integration
consequences: Enabling user-generated missions with official moderation fosters creativity but requires significant oversight infrastructure. This could slow content approval processes during peak submission periods.
options: Enable user-generated missions with official moderation
Restrict modding to visual assets only
Develop a collaborative storytelling platform for player contributions
review: Moderation demands increase operational costs, leaving collaborative storytelling tools insufficient for large-scale participation.

name: Legal Risk Mitigation Strategies
consequences: Avoiding realistic depiction of illegal activities through stylized violence reduces legal exposure but diminishes thematic authenticity. This could weaken the game's core narrative premise.
options: Avoid realistic depiction of illegal activities through stylized violence
Implement content warnings and age restrictions for sensitive themes
Partner with legal experts to pre-vet all in-game scenarios
review: Stylized violence risks narrative dilution, leaving sensitive theme accessibility unaddressed for younger demographics.
